diagrams.title: Number System Conversion Table
diagrams.description: A visual reference table showing the equivalent values of numbers 0-15 in Binary, Decimal, and Hexadecimal systems, with color-coded grouping to highlight patterns and make conversions intuitive.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the relationship between the three most important number systems in computer architecture (binary, decimal, hexadecimal) and helps students visualize patterns for quick conversions

diagrams.title: Signed Number Representation: Two's Complement
diagrams.description: Visual diagram showing how signed integers are represented using two's complement notation, demonstrating the range for n-bit numbers and the conversion process from positive to negative values.
elements: Number line showing positive and negative range
diagrams.purpose: Explains two's complement representation, the standard method for representing signed integers in computers, showing why it eliminates duplicate zero and simplifies arithmetic operations

diagrams.title: Floating-Point Number Structure (IEEE 754)
diagrams.description: Diagram showing the 32-bit IEEE 754 floating-point format with sign bit, exponent field, and mantissa (fraction) field, along with example breakdown of a decimal number into these components.
elements: Example number breakdown with bit values
diagrams.purpose: Explains how computers represent real numbers in floating-point format, understanding precision, range limitations, and the components that affect numerical accuracy in computations
